description_long: This bungalow features a low, wide profile with a prominent hip roof covered in reddish-brown tiles and a slight overhang. The exterior walls are finished in white, with a series of small, rectangular windows lining the longer sides of the building. The front facade includes a recessed entryway, a planter with greenery, and a large window, adding visual interest and a welcoming touch. The structure sits on a tiled platform that extends around the perimeter, suggesting a paved patio or walkway. The roof's deep eaves provide shade and architectural definition, while the overall design emphasizes simplicity and functionality. The building's single-story layout and modest ornamentation are characteristic of practical, suburban residential architecture.
description: A single-story white bungalow with a red-tiled hip roof, small rectangular windows, and a tiled perimeter platform. The front features a recessed entry, planter, and large window.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, a large window, a planter, and the tiled platform.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a row of small, evenly spaced windows and the extended hip roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single small window and the continuation of the tiled platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another row of small windows and a wall-mounted feature near the corner.
